text: Dive schedule confirmed for this week: Tuesday and Thursday, 8 AM–2 PM, Kāneʻohe Bay. Tank refills done, all gear checked. @l.roach you on both days?
Yes, I'll be there both days. Bringing the transect tapes and extra slates. Anyone else joining Thursday?
I can join Thursday to collect water quality samples at the same stations. Will bring the YSI.
Tuesday dive debrief: 4 of 6 transects completed at Sites 3–6. Avg coral cover ~32%, notable bleaching patches near Site 4. Full data uploaded to shared drive.
Good work. I'll flag Site 4 as priority for the Thursday survey. Safety briefing at 7:45 AM at the boat launch.
Updated GIS layers for Kāneʻohe Bay are live on the shared server: /data/gis/kaneohe_2026_q2. Includes new benthic classification raster and updated bathymetry contours.
Thanks David! Quick question — I'm seeing a salinity anomaly around Station 7 in last week's YSI export. Values dropping to ~30 ppt on Tuesday afternoon. Is that showing up in your spatial layers?
Good catch. I don't have that in the current raster — it would be a point anomaly. @t.vega can you confirm if the YSI logged a freshwater input event at Station 7?

Confirmed — there was a brief freshwater pulse at Station 7 around 1:15 PM Tuesday, likely runoff from the rain the night before. I'll add a flag to the water quality dataset and send Lorraine the corrected export.
Volunteer roster for the 1st Saturday reef monitoring (June 7) is almost full — 18 of 20 spots taken. Still need 2 certified snorkelers. @l.roach are you available to lead the morning orientation?
Yes, I can lead the orientation. Should we plan for 7:30 AM at Sand Island? I'll prep the ID cards and species sheets.
Perfect — 7:30 AM works. I'll send the confirmed volunteers a reminder email with the meetup location today.
Heads up: 3rd Saturday (June 21) roster is open for sign-ups. Last event had great turnout — 19 volunteers completed all 5 monitoring stations. Link to sign-up form in thread.
Amazing turnout! I can help coordinate the June 21 event too. Let me know if you need a second team lead for the deeper snorkel sites.
Team — the Pacific MPA policy brief draft is due October 16, 2026. I've set up a shared doc in Drive. @l.roach please draft the section on coral bleaching trends and recovery rates by Sept 30.

Noted — I'll have the bleaching trends section ready by Sept 30. I'll draw on our 2024–2026 transect data and include the Site 4 anomaly findings. Should I also add a section on thermal stress thresholds?

Grant update: the NOAA Coral Reef Conservation Program application is under review — decision expected by August 15. If funded, it would cover fieldwork costs through Q1 2027 and support the policy brief dissemination.
Team announcement: our data review meeting is scheduled for October 2, 2026 at 10 AM HST. We will walk through Q3 field results and finalize priorities for Q4. Please block your calendars.
Blocked! Will have the latest transect summaries ready to present.
🎉 Today marks Lorraine Roach's 3-year work anniversary at Coral Futures Institute! Thank you for your dedication to our reef monitoring program.
Congratulations, Lorraine! Three years of outstanding fieldwork and science. The institute is better for having you on the team.
Happy anniversary! Best dive buddy in the bay 🤿
Thank you all so much — truly, this team makes it all worthwhile. Looking forward to many more seasons in the water together!
Marisol — let's sync before the board meeting. The NOAA grant decision timeline and Q4 budget projections need to be aligned. Can we meet Friday at 2 PM?
Friday 2 PM works for me. I'll pull together the grant pipeline summary and updated budget scenarios ahead of time.
